message.content: Adversaries may compromise email accounts to further their operations, leveraging them for activities such as Phishing for Information or general phishing campaigns. By using a compromised email account, adversaries can exploit the trust associated with the existing persona, making their malicious activities more convincing. Methods for compromising email accounts include phishing for credentials, purchasing credentials from third-party sites, or brute-forcing passwords, often using data from previous breaches. Before targeting specific accounts, adversaries may conduct reconnaissance to identify valuable targets. Notable groups like APT28, APT29, HEXANE, IndigoZebra, Kimsuky, Leviathan, and Magic Hound have all used compromised email accounts for various malicious purposes, including sending spearphishing emails and conducting social engineering attacks. Mitigation strategies include implementing multi-factor authentication, monitoring for unusual login activities, educating users on recognizing phishing attempts, and regularly updating and securing passwords.
